# Test Scenarios

## Source

**GitHub Project:** `chiranb123` / Project #1  
**Issue:** #2 — *Purchase a Product Successfully*  
**Status filter:** Ready For QA  
**Target application:** SauceDemo (`https://www.saucedemo.com`)  
**Scenarios generated by:** LLM (Meta Llama 3.3 70B via Groq)

---

## Acceptance Criteria (from GitHub Issue #2)

| AC | Description |
|---|---|
| AC1 | User can log in to the application with valid credentials |
| AC2 | Products page is displayed with page title containing "Products" |
| AC3 | The list of available products is displayed on the Products page |
| AC4 | User can add a product to the shopping cart |
| AC5 | Cart badge updates to reflect the number of items added |
| AC6 | User can proceed to checkout from the cart page |
| AC7 | Checkout information form accepts First Name, Last Name, and Postal Code |
| AC8 | Order confirmation page is displayed with "Thank you for your order!" message |
| AC9 | An error message is displayed when invalid credentials are entered |
| AC10 | Checkout information validation — mandatory fields must not be empty |

---

## Scenario 1 — Happy Path: Successful Login

**Type:** Positive  
**ACs covered:** AC1, AC2

```gherkin
Scenario: Happy Path: Successful Login and Purchase
  Given the user is on the login page
  When the user enters a valid username
  And the user enters a valid password
  And the user clicks the Login button
  Then the Products page is displayed
  And the list of available products is displayed
```

**Execution result:** ✅ PASSED  
**Credentials resolved:** `standard_user` / `secret_sauce` — read from page hints at runtime  
**Verification:** Text `"Products"` found in accessibility snapshot after login

---

## Scenario 2 — Invalid Credentials: Error Message Displayed

**Type:** Negative  
**ACs covered:** AC9

```gherkin
Scenario: Invalid Credentials: Error Message Displayed
  Given the user is on the login page
  When the user enters invalid credentials
  Then an error message is displayed
```

**Execution result:** ✅ PASSED  
**Credentials used:** `definitely_not_a_real_user_!!!` / `wrong_password_!!!` — substituted via `__INVALID_VALUE__` token  
**Verification:** Login button clicked; page state captured; error message visible in final screenshot

---

## Scenario 3 — Empty Mandatory Fields: Validation Message Displayed

**Type:** Negative  
**ACs covered:** AC10

```gherkin
Scenario: Empty Mandatory Fields: Validation Message Displayed
  Given the user is on the checkout page
  When the user leaves all mandatory fields empty
  Then a validation message is displayed
```

**Execution result:** ✅ PASSED  
**Notes:** Scenario navigates to `START_URL`, submits without filling fields, captures the validation state

---

## Scenario 4 — Complete Product Purchase End to End

**Type:** Positive (end-to-end)  
**ACs covered:** AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC6, AC7, AC8

```gherkin
Scenario: Complete Product Purchase End to End
  Given the user is on the login page
  When the user enters a valid username
  And the user enters a valid password
  And the user clicks the Login button
  Then the Products page is displayed
  When the user clicks the Add to cart button
  And the user clicks the shopping cart link
  And the user clicks the Checkout button
  And the user enters "Test" in the First Name field
  And the user enters "User" in the Last Name field
  And the user enters "12345" in the Postal Code field
  And the user clicks the Continue button
  And the user clicks the Finish button
  Then "Thank you for your order!" is displayed
```

**Execution result:** ❌ FAILED (partial — login and add-to-cart passed; cart navigation locator failed after 3 self-heal attempts)  
**Credentials resolved:** `standard_user` / `secret_sauce` — read from page hints at runtime  
**Checkout details:** First Name `Test`, Last Name `User`, Postal Code `12345`  
**Failure reason:** The element locator (`llama-3.1-8b-instant` fallback model) confused "cart link" in the header with the "Add to cart" product button. The self-healing mechanism retried 3× with simplified targets but could not resolve the cart navigation link. Full screenshot and step-by-step log captured in evidence.

---

## Execution Summary

| # | Scenario | Type | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Happy Path: Successful Login and Purchase | Positive | ✅ PASSED |
| 2 | Invalid Credentials: Error Message Displayed | Negative | ✅ PASSED |
| 3 | Empty Mandatory Fields: Validation Message Displayed | Negative | ✅ PASSED |
| 4 | Complete Product Purchase End to End | Positive (E2E) | ❌ FAILED (cart nav locator) |

---

## How Scenarios Are Generated

Scenarios are **not manually written**. The pipeline is:

1. The agent fetches the GitHub issue body and extracts all Acceptance Criteria using `ACExtractor`
2. `RequirementAnalyzer` (LLM) converts the ACs into a structured `RequirementAnalysis` (user actions, expected outcomes, validations, edge cases)
3. `ScenarioGeneratorAgent` (LLM) generates up to 3 Gherkin scenarios from the analysis — always starting with the happy path
4. Scenario 4 (complete E2E purchase) was added manually to demonstrate a multi-page flow

The LLM is constrained by strict anti-hallucination rules — it may not invent credentials, page names, or element labels not present in the input. See `ANTI_HALLUCINATION.md` for details.

---

## Evidence

Full execution evidence is in the `evidence/` folder:

| File | Description |
|---|---|
| `evidence/reports/report_*.html` | Self-contained HTML dashboard with embedded screenshots |
| `evidence/reports/traceability_issue_2.json` | AC-to-scenario-to-result traceability matrix |
| `evidence/reports/*.json` | Per-scenario execution logs |
